title: GARCH/EGARCH/GJR one-step-ahead vol forecast for triple-barrier widths (Paso 2)
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: Barriers are ALREADY ATR-scaled (tp_mult*atr_v[i]); the question is replacing the contemporaneous ATR with a one-step-AHEAD forecast so barriers breathe with EXPECTED not trailing vol (point-in-time clean by construction). quant-phd-advisor: HAR-RV is the consensus best vol forecaster BUT needs intraday RV -> dead on D1; on H4 (6 bars/day) a GJR/EGARCH sigma-hat is the realistic option. Asymmetry term matters for XAU risk-off spikes.
value_rationale: VERDICT: Adopt-with-caveats, Paso 2 (H4) ONLY — defer on Paso 1.
scope: On H4: GJR/EGARCH one-step-ahead sigma-hat (data <= t-1) feeding tp_mult/sl_mult
CRITICAL: the realistic comparison is GARCH-sigma-hat vs the EXISTING ATR widths, not vs a naive fixed baseline — marginal edge may be small
Cheap offline pre-test before pipeline surgery: barrier-hit asymmetry + meta-OOS Sharpe (ATR-width vs GARCH-width) on one H4 asset, one WF split
Adopt only if it lifts median fold Sharpe AND doesn't starve folds below 30 trades
recommendation: Defer to Paso 2; run the cheap offline pre-test first. HAR-RV is off the table on D1/H4 (needs clean intraday RV).
out_of_scope: Paso 1 / D1 (cannot build RV from a daily bar; ATR already does the job). HMM statistical regimes (E1): Skip on Paso 1 — Viterbi/posterior SMOOTHING uses the full path = look-ahead invisible to current tests; only forward-filtered + walk-forward-refit is live-safe.
